benchmark_type: GPU execution timing — calibration measurement artifact

kernel_identity.digest_match: EXACT — GPU kernel produces identical digests to C99 reference

gpu_medium_v2.calibration_role: POSITIVE CONTROL CANDIDATE for gpu-medium-v2 (T3 floor, 16 GB)

analysis.gemm_dominance: GEMM accounts for 75-80% of GPU computation time across all profiles
analysis.sha256_bottleneck: CPU SHA-256 is 40-50% of total stage time; GPU-accelerated SHA-256 could halve total time
analysis.timing_stability: < 3% variance across runs — extremely tight distribution
analysis.memory_scaling: GPU total scales linearly with M (proportional to working set)
analysis.separation_signal: GPU is 100-125x faster than CPU. A device that cannot fit the working set in VRAM falls back to CPU-speed, producing a timing gap of 100x+ — far exceeding any reasonable deadline band

calibration_implications.this_card_for_gpu_small_v2: T3 data point only. Not the positive control (wrong tier). Establishes T3 baseline timing.
calibration_implications.this_card_for_gpu_medium_v2: POSITIVE CONTROL CANDIDATE. 16 GB = T3 floor. Measured timing directly usable for deadline formula.
calibration_implications.12gb_card_for_gpu_medium_v2: User has a second machine with 12 GB GPU (T2 ceiling). This would be the NEGATIVE CONTROL for gpu-medium-v2. Testing pending.

evidence_status: BENCHMARK DATA — not a gate closure. This is standalone GPU timing without the challenge protocol. Protocol timing (checkpoint_received_at - stage_issued_at) will be higher by network RTT + serialization overhead.
